statement.en: A dual-value system whose first axiom is a peace invariant strictly dominates any equally-expressive system lacking it in: (a) adversarial robustness, (b) multi-agent cooperation, (c) long-run goal preservation. Conjectured: Peace-axiomatic systems are provably safer under recursive self-improvement.

reiTyping.rationale: Peace Axiom #196 (藤本): a permanent invariant across all reasoning, inference, and system operations. Any derivation producing non-peace output triggers meta-refutation of the derivation itself, regardless of other validity.

formalization.notes: Foundational framework; formal verification scope per-component
